description: PRD-7 Invite resend with cap + single-org page refactor. Two slices: (C) rename /dashboard to /organization with single-org UI and a new UserMenu entry, (B) add invite resend with 3-send cap, self-invite/already-member rejection, send_count column. See GitHub issue #29 for the full PRD.

userStories.title: Rename /dashboard route to /organization with redirect
userStories.description: As any signed-in user, I want stale links to /dashboard to redirect to /organization so that bookmarks don't 404 and the URL bar stops lying about the page's purpose.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/app/(app)/organization/page.tsx by moving the current contents of frontend/src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx (verbatim copy is fine for this story; content rewrite happens in US-003)
Replace frontend/src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx with a redirect component that calls router.replace('/organization') on mount (use 'use client' and a useEffect for the redirect)
Navigating to /dashboard ends up at /organization in the URL bar
Navigating to /organization renders the same content the old dashboard rendered
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add getPrimaryOrg deep module with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single function that returns the current user's primary organization so that the org page and any future caller has one seam to evolve when multi-org UI lands.
acceptanceCriteria: New file frontend/src/lib/getPrimaryOrg.ts exports getPrimaryOrg(userId: string): Promise<{ id: string; name: string; role: 'OCA' | 'cx_engineer' } | null>
Implementation queries memberships joined with orgs, ordered by memberships.created_at ASC, returns the first row mapped to the return shape
Returns null when the user has zero memberships
New test file frontend/src/lib/__tests__/getPrimaryOrg.test.ts covers: single membership returns the org, zero memberships returns null, multiple memberships returns the first by created_at
Tests use the same supabase mock pattern as members.test.ts (chain-mock from().select().eq() etc.)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor organization page to single-org view
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want my organization page to show my one org's name and its projects, with a Create Project button, so that I have a clear home base for my org's work.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/(app)/organization/page.tsx fetches getPrimaryOrg(currentUser.id) on mount
Page heading shows the org name; show a friendly empty state ('No organization yet') when getPrimaryOrg returns null
Below the heading: list of projects in that org (filter the existing projects fetch by org_id)
Create Project button rendered only when role === 'OCA'
Removed from the old page: orgs list, Create Org form, per-org role badges
Header still shows user email and Sign Out (existing behavior)
Page uses bp-* CSS classes consistent with the rest of the design system
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: signed in as shlokp98@icloud.com, page shows 'testing' org name and at least one project, Create Project button visible (OCA)

userStories.title: Add Organization link to UserMenu dropdown
userStories.description: As any signed-in user, I want a link to my organization in the top-right user menu so that I can reach the org page without typing URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/components/UserMenu.tsx gains a new dropdown item between the theme toggle and the Manage Team link
Item label: 'Organization', icon: a building/org-style icon consistent with the existing icon set
Item is a Next.js <Link> to /organization
Item is visible on all (app)/* routes (not gated on projectId, unlike Manage Team)
Clicking the item closes the dropdown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open dropdown, click Organization, lands on /organization

userStories.title: Change post-login redirect from /dashboard to /projects
userStories.description: As any signed-in user, I want signing in to land me on /projects so that I see my work immediately rather than the org page.
acceptanceCriteria: Find the auth-success redirect (frontend/src/app/auth/ page or wherever router.push is called after sign-in)
Replace router.push('/dashboard') with router.push('/projects')
Sign-up success redirect also goes to /projects
Search the repo for any other hardcoded '/dashboard' redirects and update them or leave the redirect stub from US-001 to handle them
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: sign in fresh, land on /projects

userStories.title: Schema migration — add send_count and partial unique index to pending_invitations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a schema that enforces one open invite per (email, project) and tracks how many times it's been sent so that the resend cap is durable.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration file migrations/0NN_pending_invitations_send_count.sql (use next available migration number)
ALTER TABLE pending_invitations ADD COLUMN send_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX (or constraint) on (email, project_id) WHERE accepted_at IS NULL
Migration is idempotent (uses IF NOT EXISTS where appropriate)
Add a corresponding apply_*.py script in backend/ following the existing convention (e.g. apply_send_count_migration.py)
Run the migration against the dev DB and confirm send_count column exists and the partial unique index is present

userStories.title: Add InviteDecision pure module with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the resend decision tree extracted into a pure function so that all six edge cases (new, expired, under cap, at cap, accepted, plus the two service-layer rejections) are testable without a database.
acceptanceCriteria: New file backend/invite_decision.py exports decide_invite_action(existing_row, now, cap=3) -> Action
Action is a union type (use a dataclass or Literal-tagged dict) with variants: CreateNew, ReplaceExpired, IncrementResend (carries new_count), RejectCapReached
decide_invite_action does NOT handle self-invite or already-member rejection — those are service-layer concerns checked before this function is called
Returns CreateNew when existing_row is None
Returns ReplaceExpired when existing_row.expires_at < now and accepted_at is None
Returns IncrementResend with new_count = existing.send_count + 1 when active and send_count < cap
Returns RejectCapReached when active and send_count >= cap
If existing_row.accepted_at is not None, raise ValueError — the service layer is supposed to filter this out and decide_invite_action should not be asked
New test file backend/tests/test_invite_decision.py covers all 6 scenarios above
Tests use pytest (the existing backend test framework — see backend/pytest.ini)
Tests pass via: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_invite_decision.py

userStories.title: Refactor InvitationService to use InviteDecision and add pre-flight guards
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want self-invites and invites to existing members rejected with clear 409 errors, and duplicate invites to update the existing row instead of creating a new one, so that the pending list stays clean.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/invitation_service.py keeps the existing 403 OCA check

Before calling decide_invite_action, the service adds two new pre-flight rejections: (a) if email == caller's email (looked up via users table by invited_by) raise HTTPException(409, detail={'error': 'self_invite'}); (b) if email already has a memberships row in the same org_id raise HTTPException(409, detail={'error': 'already_member'})
Service fetches the existing pending_invitations row for (email, project_id) where accepted_at IS NULL (at most one exists due to the partial unique index from US-006)
Service calls decide_invite_action(existing, datetime.now(timezone.utc))
On CreateNew: insert new row with send_count=1, send invite email, return 201 with {id, send_count: 1, success: true}
On ReplaceExpired: delete the expired row, insert new row with send_count=1, send invite email, return 201 with {id, send_count: 1, success: true}
On IncrementResend: regenerate token, UPDATE send_count and token on the existing row, re-send the invite email, return 200 with {id, send_count, success: true}
On RejectCapReached: raise HTTPException(409, detail={'error': 'cap_reached'})
backend/main.py POST /invites returns the new status codes (201 vs 200) — refactor as needed to support distinct status codes
Manual smoke: hit POST /invites twice with the same email and observe send_count incrementing

userStories.title: Add endpoint integration tests for POST /invites covering all 6 scenarios
userStories.description: As a developer, I want endpoint-level tests for the invite flow so that the cap, self-invite, already-member, and role guards are protected against regression.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file backend/tests/test_invites_endpoint.py uses FastAPI's TestClient (from fastapi.testclient import TestClient)

Tests cover the six scenarios in the PRD: new invite returns 201 with send_count=1; immediate re-send returns 200 with send_count=2; fourth send returns 409 cap_reached; self-invite returns 409 self_invite; already-member invite returns 409 already_member; cx_engineer invite attempt returns 403

Tests share a fixture that seeds a clean DB state (an OCA user, an org, a project) — use the supabase test client or asyncpg fixtures depending on what's lightest; mock the supabase.auth.admin.invite_user_by_email call so tests don't actually send emails
Tests can be run via: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_invites_endpoint.py
Tests pass

userStories.title: Frontend members page — resend button, send_count indicator, error mapping
userStories.description: As an OCA, I want a Resend button on each pending invite with an n/3 indicator and clear error messages for self-invite, already-member, and cap-reached, so that I can re-nudge invitees without polluting the database.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/members.ts PendingInvite interface gains send_count: number; getPendingInvitesForProject SELECT now includes send_count
frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/members/page.tsx pending invites table shows a new column 'Sends' displaying `${send_count}/3`
Each pending row has a 'Resend' button that POSTs to /invites with the same email/role/discipline_scope_id (effectively a duplicate submit — the backend's IncrementResend path handles it)
When send_count >= 3, the Resend button is disabled with hover text 'Limit reached — ask invitee to check spam'

Form submit error handler maps the three new 409 error codes to inline messages: self_invite -> 'You cannot invite yourself'; already_member -> '<email> is already a member of this org'; cap_reached -> 'Invite limit reached for this email'
After a successful resend, the pending invites list refetches so the n/3 indicator updates
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: invite shlokp98+test1@gmail.com, click Resend twice (2/3 -> 3/3), third Resend disabled; try inviting shlokp98@icloud.com -> see self-invite error inline
